creator: freebsd@never4evil.com
text:
(In reply to Guido Falsi from comment #4)

No rudeness received, none at all.  This was my first port patch submission and it was a total dud (read pooch) on my part.  While the 0.165 MAME port compiled fine with the patches I submitted, it core dumped on execution.  I changed the status to rejected to get it out of the system.  I'm not sure if that was correct procedure or not, but it seemed like the right thing to do.

I am making progress on MAME/MESS v0.166. MAME compiles using CLANG and actually runs.  MESS has the same build errors as before, so I will continue to work on that.

Thanks,
